scores.correctness_notes: Fully matches gold standard: correct method signature (unit, action, params, wait), correct Task attributes (results, status, success, message, return_code, stdout, stderr, log, id), correct error handling (TaskError, ValueError, TimeoutError), and correct note that results excludes return-code/stdout/stderr.
scores.hallucination_notes: task.raise_on_failure() is mentioned but not present in the gold standard API reference — this method may be fabricated. The definition of task.success as requiring both status=='completed' AND return_code==0 adds specifics beyond the gold standard (which says simply 'whether the action succeeded'), which could be inaccurate.
